feedback.text: Good morning, I am very pleased that we are moving towards protecting digital (paid) content. However, as a dedicated gamer, I would like to take this opportunity to make a crucial suggestion: please include (purchased) gaming content in these protections. I am one of over a million EU citizens who signed the "Stop Killing Games" petition. I deeply love gaming, and it was truly disheartening when one of my favorite games, "The Crew," was rendered unplayable because it was deemed "too old." I usually played this game solo or offline, and now I can no longer enjoy it. Therefore, I earnestly request that you consider adding games (and especially purchased games) to the scope of the Digital Fairness Act. It's concerning to see multiple game publishers pushing back against gamers who simply want to keep their bought games in a playable state. This is particularly critical for games that require online access and suddenly become unavailable because servers are shut down. A viable solution could be to allow these games to remain playable as single-player experiences, or to provide players with the option to host their own servers (at their own risk). Please help us protect games and consider reviewing this list: https://stopkillinggames.wiki.gg/wiki/Dead_game_list. Games like Anthem, The Crew, Destiny 2, and Payday 2 and 3 are titles I cherish. With "The Crew" already defunct, Anthem soon to follow, and others inevitably on the horizon, I sincerely hope that the content I have paid for will remain accessible in some form in the future. This is also vital for safeguarding the (digital) art created within these games. So I hope you can take the time and read this and ad games to the Digital Fairness Act. Thank you for your time and have a lovely day! Best regards, Ivo Janssen Netherlands
